summary: Today's lead story: "Perturbation-Based Uncertainty for Failure Detection in Vision-Language-Action Models" (arXiv cs.RO). Coverage spans robotics_vla, jobs with 416 items scanned and 5 surfaced across sections.

items.title: Perturbation-Based Uncertainty for Failure Detection in Vision-Language-Action Models
items.summary: arXiv:2606.20754v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have shown strong performance in robotic manipulation, but reliable uncertainty quantification remains challenging, particularly under distribution shift. Unlike autoregressive policies, many modern VLA models generate continuous actions through regression or flow-based generation, where explicit predictive probabilities are unavailable. Moreover, existing approaches often rely on stochastic action sampling or s…

items.title: SafeDojo: Safe Reinforcement Learning for VLA via Interactive World Model
items.summary: arXiv:2606.20698v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safe control is a prerequisite for real-world embodied intelligence, for which safe reinforcement learning has emerged as a promising paradigm. However, existing safe reinforcement learning methods either require costly real-world exploration or depend on hand-crafted safety functions. Neither scales to vision-language-action models deployed in open-world physical environments. We propose SafeDojo, the first model-based safe reinforcement learning…

items.title: World Action Models: A Survey
items.summary: arXiv:2606.20781v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: World Action Models (WAMs) are embodied predictive-action models that make a forecast of the future available to action. Recent WAMs repurpose large video generation models, and a parallel line relies on language or vision-language backbones without a video-generation core. This rapid expansion has blurred the boundary among broad world models, video generation models, action-grounded video world models, Vision-Language-Action policies, and WAMs.…

items.title: TACT-ful: Multi-Channel Terrain Affordance and Compliance Training for Payload-Robust Perceptive Humanoid Locomotion
items.summary: arXiv:2606.20645v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foothold selection on structured terrain requires explicit reasoning about contact planarity, surface steepness, and kinematic reachability, properties not captured by a single height-based terrain signal. items.title: AbbVie’s $11B Apogee Acquisition Brings an Eczema Drug With an Edge
items.summary: Apogee Therapeutics brings AbbVie a long-acting antibody ready for Phase 3 testing in atopic dermatitis, also called eczema. This therapy’s pipeline in a product potential includes respiratory conditions such as asthma — as a monotherapy and as part of novel drug combinations. The post AbbVie’s $11B Apogee Acquisition Brings an Eczema Drug With an Edge appeared first on MedCity News .

items.title: Sector Snapshot: Robotics Startups On Fire As Venture Funding Surges To Record Numbers In 2026
items.summary: Globally, robotics startups have so far raised $18.8 billion in 2026, compared to $15 billion in the full year of 2025. The figure also handily surpasses the $14.1 billion raised in the peak venture funding year of 2021, and we still have more than six months of fundraising left. We use Crunchbase data to see where the funding went.
